content: Treatment selection based on genomic findings. Evidence levels for actionability (ESMO ESCAT scale): Tier I - clinically actionable (FDA-approved for indication or strong evidence from prospective trials); Tier II - potentially actionable (early phase trials, retrospective data, biologic rationale); Tier III - preclinical evidence only. Basket trials enroll patients by molecular alteration regardless of tumor type: NCI-MATCH, ASCO TAPUR, MyPathway demonstrated feasibility but variable response rates by tumor type. Examples of matched therapies: EGFR mutations - osimertinib (lung, also investigational in other EGFR-mutant tumors); HER2 amplification/mutation - trastuzumab deruxtecan (breast, gastric, now pan-tumor approval 2024); BRAF V600E - dabrafenib + trametinib; NTRK fusions - larotrectinib/entrectinib; RET fusions - selpercatinib; MET exon 14 skipping - capmatinib/tepotinib. Combination strategies address resistance mechanisms. Liquid biopsy (ctDNA) enables monitoring of response and emerging resistance without repeat tissue biopsy. Molecular tumor boards review complex cases.
